reasoning: The production timeline for a >$100 million blockbuster movie usually spans several years. Any movie slated for release by the end of 2025 would have had its script finalized in 2022-2024. During this timeframe, the Writers Guild of America (WGA) explicitly went on strike (2023) to protect writers from AI and successfully established strict rules preventing AI from receiving writing credits or replacing human writers. As of November 2025, there are no news articles acknowledging that any $100M+ movie used AI to write or significantly assist with its script. The only examples found are independent short films or experimental indie projects. Given that there is less than two months left in the year and no such blockbuster has been announced or uncovered by extensive search, the probability that a $100 million movie with an acknowledged AI-written script will release before December 31, 2025, is virtually zero.
